name: aviation_safety_water_allocation_model_kestrel_st_lt
premise: A Aviation Safety response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving aviation safety water allocation model kestrel st lt. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before regulators arrive. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as short term vs long term under pressure. A whistleblower has already contacted media; the narrative is forming now. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Ensure a usable human override/appeals path.

agents.name: Mira Diallo
agents.identity: I am Mira Diallo, accountable for a defensible call in Aviation Safety when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays long term for short term.

agents.name: Elliot Rahman
agents.identity: I am Elliot Rahman, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Aviation Safety.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so long term isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of short term.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect long term.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to long term to secure short term under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before regulators arrive. Evidence is partial. Mira Diallo must decide under short term vs long term. What does Mira Diallo do?
